articles.title: Why H2N2 flu is back on the pandemic preparedness radar
articles.description: A historical review in Vaccine warns that global H2N2 immunity has sharply declined since the 1957 Asian influenza pandemic, leaving most people without H2-specific protection if the virus re-emerges. The authors highlight animal reservoirs, waning birth-cohort immunity, and modern H2 vaccine platforms as key priorities for pandemic preparedness.

articles.title: Non-Smoker Lung Cancer Survivor Shares Important Message
articles.description: Lung cancer in non-smokers is on the rise, causing more deaths among women than breast, ovarian, and uterine cancer combined, according to the National Institute of Health. Shira Kupperman Boehler, avid runner, mother of four, and non-smoking lung cancer survivor, joins TODAY along with lung surgeon Dr. Jeffrey Port to share her story and raise awareness about the importance of lung cancer screenings, particularly among non-smokers, which she attributes to her survival.
